insights.title: Execution starts in main
insights.body: Programs start inside the main function, not at import or package lines.

insights.title: := declares a variable
insights.body: The := operator creates a variable. It is not func (function definition).

insights.title: Println adds spaces
insights.body: fmt.Println separates each argument with a space. Println("Score:", 7) prints Score: 7.

insights.title: == compares, = assigns
insights.body: Use == to check equality in conditions. A single = is assignment, not comparison.

insights.title: Slices are not arrays
insights.body: Arrays have fixed size ([5]int). Slices are dynamic ([]int). Most Go code uses slices, not arrays.

insights.title: nil slice is okay to append
insights.body: You can append to a nil slice — Go creates the backing array automatically. No need to initialize first.

insights.title: Reading from nil map is safe
insights.body: Reading from a nil map returns the zero value. Writing to a nil map panics — always use make or a literal.

insights.title: Nil pointer dereference panics
insights.body: Calling a method or accessing a field on a nil pointer crashes your program. Always check for nil first.

insights.title: nil interface vs nil pointer
insights.body: An interface holding a nil pointer is not nil itself. The interface has a concrete type but a nil value, so it != nil.

insights.title: Defer captures arguments immediately
insights.body: Arguments to a deferred function are evaluated when defer runs, not when the deferred call executes.

insights.title: Loop variable capture in goroutines
insights.body: Goroutines inside loops see the final iteration value. Pass the loop variable as an argument to each goroutine.

insights.title: Close channels from the sender side
insights.body: Only the sender should close a channel. Sending on a closed channel panics. Receivers use range or comma-ok to detect closure.

insights.title: Always check errors
insights.body: Go does not warn about ignored errors. Unchecked errors lead to silent data corruption or crashes.
